people.name: Adam
people.description: The first man, created by God from the dust of the ground in the Garden of Eden. Father of all humanity.

people.name: Eve
people.description: The first woman, created from Adam's rib. Mother of all living.

people.name: Cain
people.description: Firstborn son of Adam and Eve. A tiller of the ground who slew his brother Abel.

people.name: Abel
people.description: Second son of Adam and Eve. A keeper of sheep whose offering pleased God. Slain by his brother Cain.

people.name: Seth
people.description: Third son of Adam and Eve, born after Abel's death. Through his line came Noah and eventually the Messiah.

people.name: Enoch
people.description: Son of Jared who walked with God and was taken up without seeing death.

people.name: Methuselah
people.description: Son of Enoch and grandfather of Noah. Lived 969 years, the longest-lived person in the Bible.

people.name: Noah
people.description: A righteous man who found grace in the eyes of the Lord. Built the ark and preserved life through the great flood.

people.name: Shem
people.description: Eldest son of Noah. Ancestor of the Semitic peoples and the line through which Abraham descended.

people.name: Ham
people.description: Son of Noah. Father of Canaan and ancestor of many nations including Egypt and Cush.

people.name: Japheth
people.description: Son of Noah. Ancestor of the Indo-European peoples who spread across many coastlands.

people.name: Abraham
people.description: Father of the faithful, called by God to leave Ur. Received the covenant promise that through his seed all nations would be blessed.

people.name: Sarah
people.description: Wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. Laughed at God's promise of a son in her old age, yet believed.

people.name: Hagar
people.description: Egyptian handmaid of Sarah who bore Ishmael to Abraham. Encountered the angel of the Lord in the wilderness.

people.name: Ishmael
people.description: Son of Abraham and Hagar. Father of twelve princes and a great nation, as God promised.

people.name: Lot
people.description: Nephew of Abraham who chose to dwell in Sodom. Rescued before its destruction.

people.name: Isaac
people.description: Son of promise born to Abraham and Sarah in their old age. The child of laughter through whom the covenant continued.

people.name: Rebekah
people.description: Wife of Isaac, chosen by God's providence at the well. Mother of twins Esau and Jacob.

people.name: Esau
people.description: Firstborn twin of Isaac and Rebekah. Sold his birthright and became father of the Edomites.

people.name: Jacob
people.description: Son of Isaac and Rebekah, twin of Esau. Wrestled with God and received the name Israel. Father of the twelve tribes.

people.name: Leah
people.description: Elder daughter of Laban and first wife of Jacob. Mother of six sons and one daughter.

people.name: Rachel
people.description: Younger daughter of Laban and beloved wife of Jacob. Mother of Joseph and Benjamin.

people.name: Reuben
people.description: Firstborn son of Jacob and Leah. Lost his birthright due to his transgression.

people.name: Simeon
people.description: Second son of Jacob and Leah. Joined with Levi in avenging their sister Dinah.

people.name: Levi
people.description: Third son of Jacob and Leah. His descendants became the priestly tribe of Israel.

people.name: Judah
people.description: Fourth son of Jacob and Leah. Ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ. The royal tribe of Israel.

people.name: Dan
people.description: Fifth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah. His tribe settled in the northernmost territory of Israel.

people.name: Naphtali
people.description: Sixth son of Jacob, born to Bilhah. His tribe settled in upper Galilee.

people.name: Gad
people.description: Seventh son of Jacob, born to Zilpah. His tribe settled east of the Jordan.

people.name: Asher
people.description: Eighth son of Jacob, born to Zilpah. His tribe was blessed with abundance and rich food.

people.name: Issachar
people.description: Ninth son of Jacob, born to Leah. His tribe was known for understanding the times.

people.name: Zebulun
people.description: Tenth son of Jacob, born to Leah. His tribe settled along the coast of northern Israel.

people.name: Joseph
people.description: Eleventh son of Jacob and firstborn of Rachel. Sold into slavery by his brothers, rose to become ruler of Egypt under Pharaoh.

people.name: Benjamin
people.description: Twelfth and youngest son of Jacob and Rachel. His mother died giving birth to him.

people.name: Dinah
people.description: Daughter of Jacob and Leah. Her story in Shechem led to the vengeance of Simeon and Levi.

people.name: Tamar
people.description: Daughter-in-law of Judah who disguised herself to ensure the continuation of her husband's line. Ancestress of David and Jesus.

people.name: Ephraim
people.description: Younger son of Joseph, blessed by Jacob with the greater blessing over his elder brother Manasseh.

people.name: Manasseh
people.description: Elder son of Joseph. His half-tribe settled on both sides of the Jordan.

people.name: Moses
people.description: The great lawgiver and prophet who led Israel out of Egyptian bondage, received the Ten Commandments at Sinai, and guided the people through forty years in the wilderness.

people.name: Aaron
people.description: Elder brother of Moses and first high priest of Israel. Spokesman before Pharaoh.

people.name: Miriam
people.description: Sister of Moses and Aaron. A prophetess who led Israel in song after crossing the Red Sea.

people.name: Pharaoh's Daughter
people.description: The Egyptian princess who rescued the infant Moses from the Nile and raised him as her own son.

people.name: Joshua
people.description: Successor of Moses who led Israel into the Promised Land. Commanded the conquest of Canaan and divided the inheritance among the tribes.

people.name: Caleb
people.description: One of two faithful spies who trusted God's promise to give Israel the land. Given Hebron as his inheritance.

people.name: Rahab
people.description: A harlot of Jericho who hid the Israelite spies and was saved when the city fell. Ancestress of David and Jesus.

people.name: Deborah
people.description: A prophetess and the only female judge of Israel. Led Israel to victory over Sisera and the Canaanites.

people.name: Gideon
people.description: A judge of Israel who defeated the Midianite army with only 300 men, using trumpets and torches.

people.name: Jephthah
people.description: A judge of Israel and mighty warrior who delivered Israel from the Ammonites. Known for his tragic vow.

people.name: Samson
people.description: A Nazirite judge of Israel blessed with supernatural strength. Fought the Philistines and died destroying their temple.

people.name: Delilah
people.description: The woman who enticed Samson to reveal the secret of his great strength, leading to his capture by the Philistines.

people.name: Ruth
people.description: A Moabite woman who showed steadfast loyalty to her mother-in-law Naomi. Married Boaz and became great-grandmother of King David.

people.name: Boaz
people.description: A wealthy landowner of Bethlehem who became the kinsman-redeemer of Ruth. Great-grandfather of King David.

people.name: Naomi
people.description: Mother-in-law of Ruth. Left Bethlehem during a famine and returned from Moab with Ruth, who showed her great loyalty.

people.name: Hannah
people.description: Mother of Samuel the prophet. Prayed fervently for a child and dedicated him to the Lord's service.

people.name: Samuel
people.description: The last judge and a great prophet of Israel. Anointed both Saul and David as kings over Israel.

people.name: Eli
people.description: High priest and judge of Israel who mentored the young Samuel. His sons' wickedness led to judgment upon his house.

people.name: Saul
people.description: First king of Israel, anointed by Samuel. A tall Benjamite who began well but fell through disobedience and jealousy of David.

people.name: Jonathan
people.description: Son of King Saul and beloved friend of David. A valiant warrior known for his deep covenant friendship.

people.name: David
people.description: The shepherd boy who slew Goliath and became Israel's greatest king. A man after God's own heart, he united the kingdom and established Jerusalem as the capital. Author of many Psalms.

people.name: Goliath
people.description: The giant Philistine champion of Gath, over nine feet tall. Slain by the young David with a sling and a stone.

people.name: Abigail
people.description: A woman of wisdom and beauty who prevented David from shedding innocent blood. Later became David's wife.

people.name: Bathsheba
people.description: Wife of Uriah the Hittite, later wife of King David. Mother of Solomon.

people.name: Absalom
people.description: Third son of David who rebelled against his father and seized the throne. Known for his handsome appearance and long hair.

people.name: Solomon
people.description: Son of David and Bathsheba. The wisest king of Israel who built the temple in Jerusalem. Author of Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Song of Solomon.

people.name: Rehoboam
people.description: Son of Solomon whose harsh rule caused the kingdom to split into Israel (north) and Judah (south).

people.name: Jeroboam
people.description: First king of the northern kingdom of Israel. Led the ten tribes in rebellion and set up golden calves at Dan and Bethel.

people.name: Ahab
people.description: King of Israel who married Jezebel and promoted Baal worship. Confronted by the prophet Elijah.

people.name: Jezebel
people.description: Phoenician princess and wife of King Ahab. Promoted Baal worship and persecuted the prophets of God.

people.name: Hezekiah
people.description: Righteous king of Judah who restored temple worship and trusted God during the Assyrian siege of Jerusalem.

people.name: Josiah
people.description: Righteous king of Judah who discovered the Book of the Law in the temple and led a great reform.

people.name: Elijah
people.description: One of the greatest prophets of Israel. Confronted the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel and was taken to heaven in a chariot of fire.

people.name: Elisha
people.description: Prophet of Israel and successor of Elijah. Received a double portion of Elijah's spirit and performed many miracles.

people.name: Isaiah
people.description: The greatest of the writing prophets. Prophesied during the reigns of four kings of Judah and foretold the coming Messiah in extraordinary detail.

people.name: Jeremiah
people.description: Called as a prophet before birth, he warned Judah of coming judgment for forty years. Wept over Jerusalem's destruction.

people.name: Ezekiel
people.description: A priest and prophet who ministered to the Jewish exiles in Babylon. Known for vivid visions including the valley of dry bones.

people.name: Daniel
people.description: A young Hebrew exile in Babylon who remained faithful to God. Interpreter of dreams, survivor of the lion's den, and receiver of apocalyptic visions.

people.name: Shadrach
people.description: One of Daniel's three companions who refused to worship Nebuchadnezzar's golden image and was thrown into the fiery furnace.

people.name: Meshach
people.description: One of Daniel's three companions who survived the fiery furnace unharmed by God's miraculous protection.

people.name: Abednego
people.description: One of Daniel's three companions in Babylon. Stood firm in faith against the golden image decree.

people.name: Nebuchadnezzar
people.description: King of Babylon who conquered Jerusalem and took the Jews into exile. Later humbled by God and acknowledged the Most High.

people.name: Hosea
people.description: Prophet commanded by God to marry the unfaithful Gomer as a living parable of God's love for wayward Israel.

people.name: Joel
people.description: Prophet who used a devastating locust plague to warn of the coming Day of the Lord and promise the outpouring of the Spirit.

people.name: Amos
people.description: A shepherd and fig-tree dresser from Tekoa called by God to prophesy against social injustice in Israel.

people.name: Jonah
people.description: The reluctant prophet who fled from God's call to preach to Nineveh. Swallowed by a great fish for three days.

people.name: Micah
people.description: Prophet from Moresheth who proclaimed justice and mercy and foretold the Messiah's birth in Bethlehem.

people.name: Nahum
people.description: Prophet who foretold the fall of Nineveh and the destruction of the Assyrian Empire.

people.name: Habakkuk
people.description: Prophet who questioned God about injustice and received the answer that the just shall live by faith.

people.name: Zephaniah
people.description: Prophet of royal descent who warned of the Day of the Lord's judgment and promised joy for the faithful remnant.

people.name: Haggai
people.description: Post-exilic prophet who urged the returned exiles to rebuild the temple and put God first.

people.name: Zechariah
people.description: Post-exilic prophet who encouraged the rebuilding of the temple through apocalyptic visions and Messianic prophecies.

people.name: Malachi
people.description: The last Old Testament prophet who confronted Israel's spiritual apathy and foretold the coming of the Lord's messenger.

people.name: Ezra
people.description: A priest and scribe who led the second group of exiles back from Babylon and restored the study of God's Law.

people.name: Nehemiah
people.description: Cupbearer to King Artaxerxes who led the rebuilding of Jerusalem's walls in just 52 days.

people.name: Esther
people.description: Jewish queen of Persia who risked her life to save her people from genocide planned by Haman.

people.name: Mordecai
people.description: Cousin and guardian of Esther who uncovered a plot against the king. Refused to bow to Haman.

people.name: Job
people.description: A righteous and wealthy man of Uz who endured immense suffering yet maintained his faith. God restored him doubly.

people.name: Jesus
people.also_known_as: Christ, Messiah, Immanuel, Son of God, Son of Man, Lamb of God
people.description: The Son of God and promised Messiah. Born of the Virgin Mary in Bethlehem. Lived a sinless life, taught with authority, performed miracles, was crucified for the sins of the world, rose from the dead on the third day, and ascended to heaven.

people.name: Mary Mother of Jesus
people.description: A young virgin of Nazareth chosen by God to bear His Son. Favoured among women, she said 'Be it unto me according to thy word.'

people.name: Joseph of Nazareth
people.description: Husband of Mary and earthly guardian of Jesus. A righteous carpenter of the house of David who obeyed God's angel.

people.name: John the Baptist
people.description: The prophet who prepared the way for Jesus. Baptized in the Jordan River and proclaimed repentance. Beheaded by Herod.

people.name: Elizabeth
people.description: Wife of Zacharias and mother of John the Baptist. A kinswoman of Mary who was filled with the Holy Ghost.

people.name: Zacharias
people.description: A righteous priest and father of John the Baptist. Struck mute for doubting the angel Gabriel's announcement.

people.name: Simon Peter
people.description: A fisherman of Galilee and leader among the twelve apostles. Confessed Jesus as the Christ. Denied Christ thrice but was restored. Became a pillar of the early church.

people.name: Andrew
people.description: Brother of Simon Peter and one of the first called apostles. Originally a disciple of John the Baptist.

people.name: James son of Zebedee
people.description: Brother of John and one of the inner circle of three apostles. First apostle to be martyred, killed by Herod Agrippa.

people.name: John the Apostle
people.description: Brother of James, son of Zebedee. Part of Jesus' inner circle. Author of the Gospel of John, three epistles, and Revelation.

people.name: Philip the Apostle
people.description: One of the twelve apostles, from Bethsaida. Brought Nathanael to Jesus.

people.name: Bartholomew
people.description: One of the twelve apostles. Jesus said of him, 'Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.'

people.name: Matthew the Apostle
people.description: A tax collector called by Jesus to become an apostle. Author of the Gospel of Matthew.

people.name: Thomas
people.description: One of the twelve apostles who initially doubted the resurrection but confessed 'My Lord and my God' upon seeing the risen Christ.

people.name: James son of Alphaeus
people.description: One of the twelve apostles, distinguished from James the son of Zebedee.

people.name: Thaddaeus
people.description: One of the twelve apostles, also called Judas (not Iscariot). Asked Jesus why He revealed Himself to disciples but not the world.

people.name: Simon the Zealot
people.description: One of the twelve apostles, distinguished by his zeal. Little is recorded of him in Scripture.

people.name: Judas Iscariot
people.description: One of the twelve apostles who betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver. Afterward he was filled with remorse and hanged himself.

people.name: Matthias
people.description: Chosen by lot to replace Judas Iscariot as one of the twelve apostles.

people.name: Paul
people.description: The apostle to the Gentiles. A Pharisee who persecuted the church until his dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus. Authored thirteen epistles and established churches throughout the Roman Empire.

people.name: Barnabas
people.also_known_as: Joseph called Barnabas, Son of Encouragement
people.description: A Levite from Cyprus who sold his field and gave to the apostles. Traveled with Paul on the first missionary journey.

people.name: Timothy
people.description: A young disciple of Paul from Lystra, son of a Jewish mother and Greek father. Paul's trusted co-worker and recipient of two pastoral epistles.

people.name: Titus
people.description: A Greek convert and trusted companion of Paul. Appointed to organize the churches in Crete.

people.name: Luke
people.description: A physician and companion of Paul. Author of the Gospel of Luke and the Acts of the Apostles.

people.name: Mark
people.description: Author of the Gospel of Mark. Accompanied Paul and Barnabas on their first missionary journey.

people.name: Silas
people.description: A leading man among the brethren in Jerusalem. Accompanied Paul on his second missionary journey and was imprisoned with him in Philippi.

people.name: Stephen
people.description: One of the first seven deacons and the first Christian martyr. Full of faith and the Holy Ghost, he was stoned to death.

people.name: Philip the Evangelist
people.description: One of the seven deacons who preached in Samaria and baptized the Ethiopian eunuch on the road to Gaza.

people.name: Mary Magdalene
people.description: A devoted follower of Jesus from whom seven devils were cast out. First witness of the resurrection.

people.name: Martha
people.description: Sister of Mary and Lazarus of Bethany. Known for her hospitality and service to Jesus.

people.name: Mary of Bethany
people.description: Sister of Martha and Lazarus. Sat at Jesus' feet and anointed Him with costly ointment.

people.name: Lazarus
people.description: Brother of Martha and Mary of Bethany. Raised from the dead by Jesus after being in the tomb four days.

people.name: Nicodemus
people.description: A Pharisee and ruler of the Jews who came to Jesus by night. Helped prepare Jesus' body for burial.

people.name: Joseph of Arimathaea
people.description: A wealthy member of the Sanhedrin and secret disciple of Jesus who provided his own tomb for Jesus' burial.

people.name: Pontius Pilate
people.description: Roman governor of Judea who presided over the trial of Jesus. Though finding no fault in Him, delivered Jesus to be crucified.

people.name: Herod the Great
people.description: King of Judea at the time of Jesus' birth. Ordered the massacre of infants in Bethlehem to eliminate the newborn King.

people.name: Herod Antipas
people.description: Son of Herod the Great and tetrarch of Galilee. Beheaded John the Baptist and mocked Jesus during His trial.

people.name: Caiaphas
people.description: The high priest who presided over the trial of Jesus and declared it expedient that one man should die for the people.

people.name: Barabbas
people.description: A notable prisoner released by Pilate instead of Jesus, as the crowd demanded.

people.name: Priscilla
people.description: Wife of Aquila and co-worker with Paul. Together they instructed Apollos more perfectly in the way of God.

people.name: Aquila
people.description: A Jewish tentmaker from Pontus. Husband of Priscilla and fellow worker with Paul in Corinth and Ephesus.

people.name: Apollos
people.description: An eloquent Jewish man from Alexandria, mighty in the Scriptures. Instructed by Priscilla and Aquila.

people.name: James brother of Jesus
people.description: Half-brother of Jesus and leader of the Jerusalem church. Author of the Epistle of James.

people.name: Jude brother of Jesus
people.description: Half-brother of Jesus and author of the Epistle of Jude. Urged believers to contend for the faith.
